2026-02-20.name: Empowering Emergency Networks through Voice Driven Semantic Perception
2026-02-20.description: This discussion explores the shift from general-purpose transcription to specialized semantic communication, highlighting the paper Voice-Driven Semantic Perception for UAV-Assisted Emergency Networks. We analyze the democratization of the edge with new model releases like the sauti-whisper-small-swh for Swahili and the ONNX-optimized Moonshine-base architecture. These advancements allow practitioners to maintain high operational reliability through intent extraction rather than simple word-leve

2026-02-19.name: mms-1b-all: Batch fine-tuning of MMS-1b
2026-02-19.description: Today’s model releases highlight the continued expansion of multilingual ASR coverage through targeted fine-tuning of foundation models. **Vitthal Bhandari** released a series of **MMS-1b** fine-tunes for underrepresented African languages including Meh (meh), Lke (lke), Led (led), and Koo (koo), demonstrating that Meta’s massively multilingual speech model can be effectively adapted for very low-resource language communities.

2026-02-18.name: Transforming Spoken Language Modeling Through Zero-Resource Syllable Tokenization
2026-02-18.description: Explore the breakthrough ZeroSyl research by Nicol Visser and Simon Malan, which introduces zero-resource syllable tokenization to dramatically improve Spoken Language Modeling efficiency. We dive into the practical side of edge AI with the new MLX quantized versions of NVIDIA’s Nemotron-Speech 0.6B model, enabling high-performance streaming ASR on consumer hardware. The discussion also covers community-driven efforts for low-resource languages like Fongbe and how phonetic IPA-specialized models

2026-02-17.name: CLAP-Based Automatic Word Naming Recognition and whisper-base-ps
2026-02-17.description: Mistral AI’s release of Voxtral-Mini-4B-Realtime-2602 is the day's most significant development, signaling a shift toward efficient, low-latency multimodal foundation models capable of integrated speech and language processing. In research, the application of Contrastive Language-Audio Pretraining (CLAP) to automatic word naming for post-stroke aphasia demonstrates how embedding-based contrastive learning can outperform traditional ASR in handling the high acoustic variability and disfluencies of pathological speech.

2026-02-16.name: From Scarcity to Scale: A and parakeet-tdt-0.6b
2026-02-16.description: Notable ASR research today includes a comprehensive analysis of the Pashto Common Voice dataset documenting its growth from 1.5 hours to 2,769 hours, and a study on enabling ASR for disordered Akan speech with a new 50-hour corpus. The DAMA framework for depth-aware model adaptation shows strong results across 18 low-resource languages with 80% fewer trainable parameters.

2026-02-22.name: ASR Highlights for February 22
2026-02-22.description: Although no major papers or models were released in this specific 24-hour window, the speech research community remains centered on the rapid evolution of large-scale speech-language models (SLMs) that integrate audio natively into the transformer backbone. Current industry trajectories are heavily prioritizing the refinement of discrete neural audio codecs to better capture paralinguistic nuances while maintaining low-latency inference for real-time conversational AI.

2026-02-21.name: whisper-large-v3: No new papers to
2026-02-21.description: Today’s developments focus on domain-specific and low-resource ASR fine-tuning, highlighted by the release of `Qwen3-ASR-0.6B-Albanian`, which demonstrates the adaptation of small-scale LLM architectures for speech tasks in under-represented languages. The continued dominance of the Whisper ecosystem is evident through several new fine-tunes targeting Indonesian, Urdu, and specialized niches such as academic content and medical transcription via the ONNX-optimized `medasr-onnx`.

2026-W08.name: Integrating Realtime Native Audio Processing into Multimodal Foundation Models
2026-W08.description: This episode explores the shift toward native audio processing with the release of Mistral AI’s Voxtral-Mini-4B-Realtime model for low-latency multimodal interaction. We examine how the CLAP framework is revolutionizing clinical speech assessments for post-stroke aphasia and discuss the efficiency gains of Depth-Aware Model Adaptation for low-resource languages. From the massive scaling of Pashto datasets to NVIDIA’s latest Parakeet updates, we analyze how the industry is moving beyond simple tr

qwen3-asr-technical-report-deep-dive.name: Scaling Multilingual Speech Recognition with the Qwen3-ASR Model Family
qwen3-asr-technical-report-deep-dive.description: This episode explores the Qwen3-ASR technical report, detailing a new family of models that bridge foundation linguistic intelligence with specialized speech recognition tasks. We dive into the modular architecture of the AuT encoder and the impressive production efficiency of the Qwen3-ASR-0.6B model. The discussion also highlights breakthrough capabilities in singing voice recognition and the innovative slot-filling approach used by the Qwen3-ForcedAligner for high-speed timestamping.

qwen3-omni-technical-report-deep-dive.name: Scaling Multimodal Speech Models Without Modality Induced Degradation
qwen3-omni-technical-report-deep-dive.description: This deep dive explores the groundbreaking release of Qwen3-Omni, a multimodal model that eliminates the modality tax while achieving state-of-the-art results on audio benchmarks. We examine the innovative Thinker-Talker MoE architecture and the Audio Transformer encoder trained on twenty million hours of data. Discover how the Code2Wav convolutional network enables ultra-low latency voice interactions and learn about the implications of the new TM-RoPE time-alignment scheme for synchronized aud

robust-speech-recognition-via-large-scal.name: How Large Scale Weak Supervision Revolutionized Robust Speech Recognition
robust-speech-recognition-via-large-scal.description: In this episode, we dive into the landmark OpenAI paper "Robust Speech Recognition via Large-Scale Weak Supervision" which introduced the world to Whisper. We explore how training on 680,000 hours of messy internet data allowed Whisper to achieve incredible zero-shot generalization across diverse audio conditions. Our guest breaks down the multitask transformer architecture and explains why prioritizing real-world robustness over LibriSpeech benchmarks shifted the entire industry toward more ver

hubert-self-supervised-speech-representa.name: Masked Prediction and Hidden Units for Self-Supervised Speech Learning
hubert-self-supervised-speech-representa.description: In this episode, we explore the landmark HuBERT paper from Meta, which revolutionized self-supervised speech representation by replacing contrastive learning with masked prediction of hidden units. We dive into the technical details of how offline K-means clustering discretizes continuous audio signals, allowing the model to achieve state-of-the-art results on the Librispeech benchmark. Discover how this iterative training approach outperformed wav2vec 2.0 and laid the foundation for modern gene

tac-timestamped-audio-captioning-deep-di.name: Solving Semantic Collapse in Complex Acoustic Scenes with TAC
tac-timestamped-audio-captioning-deep-di.description: This episode explores the TAC model from Adobe Research, a breakthrough in timestamped audio captioning designed to eliminate hallucinations in large audio language models. We discuss how the Dynamic Acoustic Mixer generates precise training data to solve semantic collapse and why the "describe-then-reason" paradigm outperforms traditional end-to-end approaches. Learn how TAC achieves state-of-the-art results on the TACOS benchmark by providing controllable, multi-resolution descriptions for com

2026-02-23.name: Optimizing Real Time Speech Recognition for Mobile Edge Devices
2026-02-23.description: This episode explores the transition of complex speech research into real-world edge computing applications through the CoreML adaptation of NVIDIA’s Parakeet-TDT architecture. We discuss the significance of phonetic transcription with the ipa-whisper-medium model and analyze how small multimodal models like Voxtral-Mini-4B are integrating speech as a primary data type. Learn how these breakthroughs in on-device processing and cultural specialization are redefining the future of voice-as-an-inte

csm-crossing-the-uncanny-valley-of-conve.name: Crossing the Uncanny Valley with the Conversational Speech Model
csm-crossing-the-uncanny-valley-of-conve.description: This episode explores the groundbreaking Conversational Speech Model from Sesame Research, a new architecture designed to overcome the robotic limitations of traditional cascaded speech systems. We discuss how the team utilized the Mimi codec and a million-hour dataset refined through a dual-Whisper strategy to achieve unprecedented vocal naturalness. Join us as we analyze technical innovations like compute amortization and evaluate how these multimodal transformers are finally bridging the gap

benchmark-datasets-deep-dive.name: ASR Benchmark Deep Dive: Choosing Models by Dataset, Not Averages
benchmark-datasets-deep-dive.description: A benchmark-first technical podcast episode covering what each major ASR dataset measures and how model rankings shift across LibriSpeech, Common Voice, VoxPopuli, TED-LIUM, GigaSpeech, SPGISpeech, Earnings22, and AMI. Includes practical guidance for selecting models for meeting transcription, finance, and open-domain workloads.

2026-02-24.name: Achieving Low Latency in Generative Speech with CTC Alignment
2026-02-24.description: This episode explores cutting-edge techniques for reducing latency in speech technology, specifically focusing on the new CTC-TTS dual-streaming framework for real-time text-to-speech. We also examine how Matryoshka Representation Learning is bridging the digital divide for languages like Wolof through efficient cross-lingual embeddings. Finally, the discussion highlights a wave of specialized model releases on Hugging Face, including Qwen3-ASR and localized Whisper fine-tunes designed for edge

2026-02-25.name: TG-ASR: Translation-Guided Learning with Parallel and WMAC
2026-02-25.description: Today’s research highlights a strong focus on enhancing low-resource speech processing through cross-modal and cross-lingual signals, exemplified by **TG-ASR**, which utilizes translation-guided learning and gated cross-attention to improve Taiwanese Hokkien ASR. This trend is echoed in the Nepali-English S2TT work on mitigating structural noise and the scaling of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) via speech-text fusion to overcome the scarcity of multilingual image-text pairs.

2026-03-09.name: ASR Highlights for March 09
2026-03-09.description: Today’s developments center on the refinement and optimization of established architectures for specialized domains, with a notable emphasis on multilingual and multimodal capabilities. The release of the `MSP-Visual-AV-HuBERT` model stands out as a significant contribution to audio-visual speech representation learning, utilizing the LRS2 dataset to advance robust ASR in noise-heavy environments.

2026-03-11.name: SCENEBench: An Audio Understanding Benchmark and w2v-bert-2.0
2026-03-11.description: Today’s research highlights a shift toward complex audio understanding beyond ASR, with benchmarks like **SCENEBench** and **MUGEN** targeting multi-audio scenarios and industrial use cases, while **ALARM** introduces alignment strategies for integrating audio with reasoning language models (RLMs). Efficiency and portability are central themes, as seen in **Speech-Omni-Lite’s** cost-efficient vision-language extensions and **SPAR-K**, a modality-aware early-exit framework that significantly accelerates inference for interleaved spoken language models.

2026-03-13.name: Scaling Real Time Audio Systems with Unified Model Architectures
2026-03-13.description: This episode explores the shift toward deployment-ready audio intelligence by examining unified streaming architectures and paralinguistic awareness in large audio language models. We dive into the Uni-ASR framework for joint transcription modes and the Hikari model’s policy-free approach to simultaneous translation. The discussion also covers the Resonate framework for audio generation and innovative ASR applications for pathological speech using clinical biomarker supervision.

2026-03-14.name: Surgicalizing Speech Technology for Localized and Efficient ASR Performance
2026-03-14.description: This episode explores the transition from massive foundational papers to practical downstream innovation and localized model fine-tuning. We dive into the technical details of specialized releases like the Quranic FastConformer and the thedal-tamil series for morphologically rich languages. The discussion also covers efficiency breakthroughs including Whisper Turbo variants and F5TTS-onnx for high-speed, cross-platform audio generation in real-world environments.

2026-03-16.name: Optimizing Multilingual Speech Models for Specialized Localized Edge Deployment
2026-03-16.description: This episode explores the industry's shift toward practical localization and edge deployment, highlighting how developers are moving beyond general foundation models to support specific dialects like Tunisian Arabic and Haryanvi. We discuss the significance of new model releases including the whisper-large-v3-turbo-ko distillation and the gigaam-v3-e2e-rnnt-onnx format for low-latency streaming. Learn why the community is prioritizing efficiency through 4-bit quantization and CPU-optimized archi

2026-03-18.name: Scaling Massively Multilingual Speech Representation With Omnilingual SONAR
2026-03-18.description: This episode explores the breakthrough Omnilingual SONAR paper which creates a unified semantic space for over 2,000 languages across text and speech. We also dive into Polyglot-Lion, a highly efficient ASR model for Singapore that achieves massive performance on a consumer budget through balanced fine-tuning of Qwen3-ASR. Finally, we discuss the RECOVER framework for agentic entity correction and the challenges of measuring emotion in synthetic speech.

2026-03-19.name: Engineering Rigor and Neuron Level Control in Audio Models
2026-03-19.description: This episode explores the transition from opaque audio models to transparent, controllable speech systems. We dive into TalTech’s Multi-Source Evidence Fusion for verifiable reasoning in audio question answering and the Zipper-LoRA framework for managing multilingual parameter decoupling. Additionally, we discuss the breakthrough discovery of Emotion-Sensitive Neurons in models like Qwen2.5-Omni, enabling training-free emotional steering. Join us to see how these advancements are productionizing

2026-03-20.name: Evolution of Audio Intelligence in Large Language Model Backbones
2026-03-20.description: This episode explores the transition from simple transcription to sophisticated audio intelligence using Large Audio Language Models. We discuss how latent auditory knowledge in backbones like Llama and Qwen shapes performance and examine the challenges of phonetic ambiguity through the APUN-Bench for puns. Finally, we analyze how ASR quality impacts clinical outcomes in Alzheimer’s detection using the ADReSSo 2021 dataset and highlight the new Qwen3-ASR-1.7B model release.

2026-03-26.name: A Sociolinguistic Analysis of Automatic and w2vbert_final
2026-03-26.description: Today's research highlights a significant focus on mitigating "contextual exposure bias" in Speech-LLMs by addressing the train-test mismatch between oracle training history and noisy inference context, a crucial step for robust conversational agents. Efforts to scale general audio understanding are also accelerating with the release of ACAVCaps, which provides the granular descriptions necessary for fine-grained audio-language modeling.

2026-04-03.name: Architectural Shifts Toward Stable and Specialized Speech Technology
2026-04-03.description: This episode explores the transition from general-purpose large models to precision engineering in speech technology. We discuss the T5Gemma-TTS technical report and its use of encoder-decoder architectures for stable long-form synthesis. Our conversation also covers EndoASR for specialized medical environments and the TRACE framework for training-free deepfake detection. Learn how domain adaptation and architectural choices are solving real-world challenges in ASR and TTS today.

2026-04-05.name: Bridging the Gap Through Specialized Edge Speech Model Deployment
2026-04-05.description: This episode explores the transition from theoretical research to practical edge deployment in the speech technology landscape. We examine how models like Whisper-3-turbo and the 120M parameter Parakeet-EOU are being optimized for CoreML and INT8 quantization to enable faster on-device inference. Additionally, the discussion covers the impact of fine-tuning XLS-R for low-resource languages like Bemba and the rise of emotional controllability in new text-to-speech frameworks.

2026-04-06.name: Optimizing Whisper Models for Specialized Domain and Hardware Deployment
2026-04-06.description: This episode explores the industrialization phase of speech technology, focusing on how developers are optimizing models like Whisper for production environments. We discuss the impact of VibeVoice-ASR-HF and NF4 quantization on inference speed, alongside the emergence of the vak-v1 small speech language model for edge-native intelligence. Learn why hardware-specific acceleration through Apple’s MLX framework and domain-specific fine-tuning are becoming the new standards for modern ASR deploymen

2026-04-08.name: Integrating Speech Foundation Models into Immersive Extended Reality Environments
2026-04-08.description: This episode explores the INTERACT framework, an AI-driven XR system utilizing Whisper and RoBERTa to provide real-time sign language interpretation on Meta Quest 3 headsets. We also discuss research into speaker diarization across the lifespan using EEND-VC to address age-related vocal differences. Finally, we examine the release of the Qwen3-ASR series and the growing impact of GGUF quantization on edge deployment for specialized speech applications.

2026-04-09.name: Cutting Redundancy for Efficient Inference in Speech Models
2026-04-09.description: This episode explores the shift toward efficiency in ASR through groundbreaking research on redundancy and memory optimization. We dive into the Affinity Pooling method for training-free token merging and the AudioKV paper's Spectral Score Smoothing technique for compressing the KV cache in models like Qwen3-Omni-30B. Listeners will also learn how Mixed Batching allows for effective domain adaptation using as little as four hours of audio data.
